query: All my desktop widgets (clock, system monitor) use :stacking "bg" and they vanish the instant I change my wallpaper with swww. eww active-windows still lists them as open. The fg-stacked bar is fine. What's happening?
expected_behavior: Routes to the ':stacking "bg" widgets disappear after a wallpaper change' error in COMMON_ERRORS.md

Explains that eww 'bg' and the wallpaper daemon (swaybg/swww/wpaperd) share the wlr-layer-shell background layer; wlroots stacks by creation order, so the new wallpaper surface lands on top and obscures the bg widgets
Prescribes switching persistent desktop widgets to `:stacking "bottom"` (above wallpaper, below normal windows)
Offers the bulk migration sed + `systemctl --user restart eww.service`
notes: Guards against chasing a phantom 'eww closed the window' bug (active-windows still lists them) when the real cause is a layer-shell stacking conflict with the wallpaper daemon. The fix is a one-word stacking change, not a daemon restart hunt.
